original_abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) has emerged as a cornerstone technology for 5G-Advanced (5G-A) and 6G networks due to its ability to improve spectral efficiency and reduce hardware overhead. By unifying high-accuracy sensing with robust data transmission, ISAC is poised to enable a wide range of intelligent applications. Since the underlying waveform dictates the upper bounds of both sensing and communication performance, this article provides a comprehensive review of ISAC signals through the lens of modern mobile communication systems. We first examine signal design strategies tailored for the evolving architectures of 5G, 5G-A, and 6G. We then explore essential radar signal processing techniques applicable to ISAC, focusing on channel information matrix estimation, spectral line estimation, and super-resolution algorithms. Furthermore, we address critical signal optimization challenges, including peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) reduction, interference management, and adaptive waveform design. By synthesizing current advancements and methodologies, this review serves as a roadmap for future research and development of ISAC signals in next-generation mobile networks.
humanized_abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is recognized as one of the key technologies for the future 5G-Advanced (5G-A) and 6G systems, aiming to increase the spectral efficiency and reduce the cost of hardware resources. Through the synergy of precise sensing and reliable communication, ISAC can implement a large number of intelligent applications. The waveform in ISAC determines the ceiling of the performance of the sensing and communication. Thus, this article is aimed at summarizing the signal design of integrated sensing and communication from the perspective of modern mobile communication systems. The signal design for 5G, 5G-A, and 6G systems and the corresponding radar signal processing techniques are introduced in the first part, including the methods of channel information matrix estimation, spectral line estimation, and super-resolution. Then, the signal optimization researches, such as PAPR reduction, interference suppression, and adaptive waveform design, are introduced in the second part. Through the summary of existing achievements and methods, this paper can guide the research and design of ISAC signals in the future mobile communication systems.
